name: Pinto Beans
recipeIngredient: 2 teaspoons chili powder, or to taste
crushed red pepper flakes -- to taste
recipeInstructions:
1. Pick through the beans to remove any rocks, dirt, or debris.
2. Rinse the beans thoroughly three times with water.
3. Cover the beans with fresh water and bring them to a boil over high heat.
4. Boil for 2 minutes, then turn off the heat and let the beans soak until they are soft (about 2 hours). Alternatively, you can soak the beans overnight in cold water after washing.
5. Add all remaining ingredients except salt and black pepper to the pot. Bring the mixture back to a boil, cover, and simmer until the beans reach your desired tenderness, about 1 1/2 hours. Add water as needed during cooking.
6. Add salt and black pepper in the last few minutes of cooking.
Note: Most ingredients are optional; adjust according to personal taste preferences. You can substitute the ham hock with 3 or 4 slices of bacon or omit both and use 2 tablespoons of oil instead. Serve with hot cornbread, chopped green onions, and fresh sliced tomatoes.
